title: campanula affinis subsp. bolosii
intro: Campanula affinis subsp. bolosii, also known as Bolos' campanula, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and it has been used to treat various conditions including bronchitis and coughs. Campanula affinis subsp. bolosii can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is sometimes used as a component in herbal remedies and supplements.
